name: Silenus
description: Silenus is a figure in Greek mythology, often associated with Dionysus, the god of wine, revelry, and fertility. He is typically depicted as an elderly, jovial satyr, often drunk and unsteady on his feet. In some accounts, he is considered the foster-father and tutor of Dionysus, imparting wisdom and guidance to the young god. Silenus is known for his profound knowledge, which he often shares when captured and forced to speak. However, his wisdom is usually delivered in a drunken, rambling manner, making it difficult to decipher. He is a symbol of the ecstatic release and liberation associated with wine and the Dionysian cult. Despite his disheveled appearance, Silenus is revered for his connection to the divine and his ability to reveal hidden truths. He is often surrounded by other satyrs, nymphs, and maenads, participating in wild celebrations and ecstatic rituals. He represents the wild, untamed aspects of nature and the blurring of boundaries between the human and divine realms. Silenus's drunken state often masks a deeper understanding of the cosmos, and his pronouncements, though seemingly nonsensical, often contain profound insights into the nature of reality. He is a complex figure, embodying both the joys and the dangers of intoxication and the pursuit of ecstatic experience. He is often shown riding a donkey or being supported by satyrs, reflecting his inebriated state and reliance on others. He is a vital component of Dionysian mythology, representing the potential for both chaos and enlightenment within the ecstatic experience.
subtitle: Drunken Sage and Companion of Dionysus

keyMyths.title: The Capture of Silenus
keyMyths.description: King Midas captured Silenus and, in exchange for returning him to Dionysus, received the golden touch. This gift ultimately proved to be a curse.

keyMyths.title: Silenus and the Birth of Dionysus
keyMyths.description: Silenus is often depicted as being present at the birth of Dionysus, and in some versions, he is even Dionysus's tutor. He played a vital role in raising the young god.

keyMyths.title: Silenus's Cosmology
keyMyths.description: When captured by King Midas, Silenus allegedly revealed a pessimistic view of the human condition: that the best thing for humans is never to be born.
